base_url.description: Microsoft Dataverse Web API base URL, e.g. https://org.crm.dynamics.com/api/data/v9.2. No default: an organization URL must be named explicitly (matches legacy's requirement for either config base_url or org_url).

login_base_url.description: Azure AD login base URL override for tests or proxies; combined with tenant_id to derive the OAuth2 token endpoint.

token_url.description: Full OAuth2 token endpoint override. When set, takes priority over the derived login_base_url/tenant_id endpoint.

scope.description: OAuth2 client-credentials scope, e.g. https://org.crm.dynamics.com/.default (Dataverse's own resource-scope convention: the scheme+host of base_url, with a /.default suffix). Legacy derives this automatically from base_url's host; the declarative template dialect has no mechanism to strip a URL's path component, so this bundle requires scope to be supplied directly instead (documented parity deviation; see docs.md Known limits).

page_size.description: Records requested per @odata.nextLink page (1-5000), sent as the $top query parameter on the first request of each stream.

max_pages.description: Optional hard cap on the number of @odata.nextLink pages read per stream, consumed by hooks/microsoft-dataverse/hooks.go's StreamHook — NOT a declarative streams.json field (pagination is entirely hook-driven since next_url_path cannot address the literal "@odata.nextLink" key; see docs.md Known limits). Permissive parse: empty, "all", or "unlimited" means unbounded.

client_id.description: Azure AD application (client) ID used for the OAuth2 client-credentials grant. Never logged.

client_secret.description: Azure AD application client secret, sent only in the OAuth2 client-credentials token-exchange request; never logged.

tenant_id.description: Azure AD tenant ID (GUID or verified domain), used to derive the per-tenant token endpoint. Marked secret to match legacy, which reads it from Secrets.

mode.description: Runtime mode: live (default) or fixture for credential-free conformance.
